findings.title: Privacy Risk: Data Handling Gaps
findings.description: Detected privacy risk keywords indicating potential gaps in PII handling and data collection practices, particularly in relation to the justification for data collection and consent mechanisms.
assumptions: Data handling practices may not align with policy requirements.
recommendations: Review and enhance data handling practices to ensure compliance with data minimization and consent requirements.

metadata.policy_rule: Require collection justification and consent mechanism.

findings.title: Authentication Risk: Weaknesses in Auth Flow
findings.description: Identified potential weaknesses in the authentication flow, particularly the lack of SSO/SAML integration, which is critical for enterprise security compliance.
assumptions: Enterprise clients require SSO/SAML for compliance.

evidence.excerpt: Risk keywords related to authentication detected.

recommendations: Implement SSO/SAML integration to enhance security compliance.

findings.title: Compliance Risk: Data Residency Controls
findings.description: Compliance risks identified due to lack of data residency controls, which are necessary to meet GDPR and data sovereignty requirements.
assumptions: Enterprise clients require strict data residency controls.
recommendations: Establish data residency controls to comply with GDPR.

findings.title: Security Risk: Data Isolation Compliance
findings.description: The current application-level row filtering for tenant data isolation poses a compliance risk, especially with SOC 2 Type II requirements.
assumptions: Proper data isolation is necessary for compliance.

evidence.excerpt: Compliance risk due to data isolation model.

recommendations: Implement schema or database-level separation for data isolation.

summary: Identified 4 risks (1 compliance, 1 privacy, 2 security). 3 flagged as blockers. Risk gate: FAILED (4 blockers, 1 warnings).
